caveat: The optimism gap is a synthetic→synthetic distribution shift (a lower severity scale within the same generative model), NOT a sim-to-field claim. Results demonstrate the phenomenon and the predictor's signal on synthetic data; they do not assert field-detection performance.

ablation_no_auc_in.note: auc_in removed. Because the target gap = auc_in − mean_OOD, auc_in is one additive term of the target; this shape-only predictor shows the NON-tautological predictability from score-distribution shape alone.

ablation_no_self_slope.note: Self-perturbation slope removed (the only generator-touching feature). Compare R² to the headline to gauge how much it carries.

permutation_null.note: Fraction of label-permuted runs whose out-of-fold R² ≥ the observed R² ((#≥obs + 1)/(n+1)). Small p ⇒ the predictability is unlikely under no ID→gap relationship.

label: MODELLED — synthetic parameter-grounded corpus (never field/IQ); AUC over model-derived labels; operating characteristics only, no good/bad verdict

learned_vs_physics.note: Mean optimism gap pooled over classes and severities, by family. learned = full-feature (logreg + MLPs); learned_single_feature = logreg on one observable. matched_dimensionality_pairs compare a single-observable physics baseline against a single-feature learned detector reading the same observable — the H2 evidence-breadth control: similar gaps within a pair ⇒ the gap is about evidence breadth, not learning.
